summary: Tabs that rewrite their own URL in-page (most visibly Google Docs churning its ?tab=t.… query string via the History API, plus any SPA #hash change) were being silently ejected from their tab group. The onUpdated handler now ejects a grouped tab only on a real navigation — a change to a different origin or pathname — and keeps it grouped when only the query string or fragment moved.

changes.title: Add a general same-page identity helper
changes.description: New src/lib/utils/samePageKey.js reduces a URL to its origin + pathname, dropping the query string and fragment. Equal keys mean an in-page rewrite (keep grouped); different keys mean a genuine navigation (eject). A non-string or unparseable value falls back to the raw input so two such values compare equal only when literally identical, preserving today's behavior for chrome://, about:blank, and empty strings. This is a structural rule, not a per-host allow-list, so it can't rot when a site changes its URL scheme.

tests.name: samePageKey › treats a query-string-only change as the same page
tests.description: A change in only the query string keeps the same origin+pathname identity.

tests.name: samePageKey › treats the Google Docs tab-query churn as the same page
tests.description: The exact reported case: ?tab=t.A and ?tab=t.B on a deep docs path compare equal.

tests.name: samePageKey › treats a fragment-only change as the same page
tests.description: A change in only the #fragment keeps the same page identity.

tests.name: samePageKey › treats a different path as a different page
tests.description: A different pathname is a genuine navigation and must not compare equal.

tests.name: samePageKey › treats a different origin as a different page
tests.description: A different origin (host) is a genuine navigation and must not compare equal.

tests.name: samePageKey › returns the origin and pathname with query and fragment stripped
tests.description: Confirms the literal output shape: origin+pathname only.

tests.name: samePageKey › falls back to identity comparison for unparseable inputs
tests.description: Unparseable values fall back to the raw input, comparing equal only when identical.

tests.name: samePageKey › returns a non-string input unchanged without throwing
tests.description: A non-string input is returned unchanged rather than throwing.

changes.title: Gate the onUpdated ungroup on a real navigation
changes.description: In service_worker.js the chrome.tabs.onUpdated handler previously ungrouped any grouped tab whose URL changed. It now derives the old URL from the stored urlKey, compares samePageKey(oldUrl) to samePageKey(changeInfo.url), and calls chrome.tabs.ungroup only when they differ (a genuine navigation). URL/visit bookkeeping (closeUrl, newUrl) is left untouched, so only the eject decision changed.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › does not ungroup a grouped tab on a query-string-only change
tests.description: Regression test for the reported Google Docs bug: a query-only change does not call chrome.tabs.ungroup.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › does not ungroup a grouped tab on a fragment-only change
tests.description: A fragment-only (SPA anchor) change does not eject the grouped tab.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › still ungroups a grouped tab that navigates to a different path
tests.description: Guards against over-correction: a different-path navigation still ejects the tab.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › still ungroups a grouped tab that navigates to a different origin
tests.description: A cross-origin navigation still ejects the tab, as before.
